name: Tim
subject: enough is enough is enough is enough
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
I am devastated that there have been another two mass shootings in our country this weekend -- this time in El Paso, Texas and in my home state, Dayton, Ohio.

Enough is enough is enough is enough.

[I know this issue is painful and if you are triggered and can't read any further, simply join my call to action to stop gun violence now »](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+I+know+this+issue+is+painful+and+if+you+&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1")

The victims of these massacres -- some of them children -- had hopes and dreams. They had plans this week to spend time with their families, to go to work, or school or church -- and now they're gone. They deserve more.

[We cannot continue to offer up the same condolences again and again with no action to protect our communities.](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+We+cannot+continue+to+offer+up+the+same+&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1")

[I'm calling on the Senate to immediately end its August recess and reconvene in Washington to take up a package of legislation meant to stop these acts of horror and other acts of gun violence that affect every single American.](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=3+-+Irsquom+calling+on+the+Senate+to+immedia&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1")

We need to pressure Mitch McConnell to immediately bring the background check bill we passed in the House weeks ago up for a vote in the Senate.

We need to pass an assault weapons ban.

We need to address gun reform as the public health crisis that it is -- that starts with more support and mental health services for our children and students.

We need to end the xenophobic, hate-fueled culture President Trump has ignited and start to heal America -- before another mother or child is massacred.

[Join my call to action now »](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=4+-+Join+my+call+to+action+now+raquo&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190804_2_1")

Tim

---
Yesterday, we woke up to the second mass shooting in just as many days. I'm heartbroken for El Paso and Dayton, and to all the families who have just endured unimaginable loss.

We need commonsense gun reform -- _now,_ before the next mass shooting, to stop the daily gun violence on our streets, and to make sure that our families and loved ones are safe.

But Mitch McConnell and Republicans in the Senate are blocking legislation that could help address this epidemic.

During the last election, we flipped the House. And in February, the Democratic-led House passed bipartisan legislation that would make a meaningful impact on this crisis.

But five months after passing the House, and more than six and a half years after Sandy Hook, that bill is still sitting on Mitch McConnell's desk. It's inexcusable.

**It's clear Republicans don't have the courage to do something about this crisis. We can't wait for them to act -- because they won't. If we're going to address the gun violence epidemic in our country, we need to take back the Senate in 2020.**

I'll fight my heart out to make sure Democrats win up and down the ballot in 2020 -- but if we're going to beat Republicans and the gun lobby, it's going to take a grassroots movement.

As long as Mitch McConnell-led Senate Republicans are in power, they will stonewall and block any attempts for change. That's because they answer to the gun lobbyists who sign their checks -- no matter how many innocent people die each and every day at the hands of their inaction.

Enough is enough. We must call out what happened in El Paso and Dayton for what they are: **domestic terrorism.** It's a threat to the United States, and we've seen its devastating toll this weekend. We need to call out the president himself for advancing racism and white supremacy. And then we need to take action.

It's powerfully important that we support Democrats across the country and take back the Senate in 2020 -- so we can take power back from Republicans, fight back against the gun lobby, and address the gun violence epidemic head on.

clean_body: It's clear Republicans don't have the courage to do something about this crisis. We can't wait for them to act -- because they won't. | | | "" Yesterday, we woke up to the second mass shooting in just as many days. I'm heartbroken for El Paso and Dayton, and to all the families who have just endured unimaginable loss. We need commonsense gun reform -- now, before the next mass shooting, to stop the daily gun violence on our streets, and to make sure that our families and loved ones are safe. But Mitch McConnell and Republicans in the Senate are blocking legislation that could help address this epidemic. During the last election, we flipped the House. And in February, the Democratic-led House passed bipartisan legislation that would make a meaningful impact on this crisis. But five months after passing the House, and more than six and a half years after Sandy Hook, that bill is still sitting on Mitch McConnell's desk. It's inexcusable. It's clear Republicans don't have the courage to do something about this crisis. We can't wait for them to act -- because they won't. If we're going to address the gun violence epidemic in our country, we need to take back the Senate in 2020. I'll fight my heart out to make sure Democrats win up and down the ballot in 2020 -- but if we're going to beat Republicans and the gun lobby, it's going to take a grassroots movement. "" | If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically and be evenly split to support the eventual Democratic nominee for Senate in AK, AR, AZ, CO, GA, IA, ID, KS, KY, LA, ME, MS, MT, NC, NE, NM, OK, SC, SD, TN, TX, WV, WY, and Doug Jones and Tina Smith: | | | | "" | "" | | "" | "" | | "" | "" As long as Mitch McConnell-led Senate Republicans are in power, they will stonewall and block any attempts for change. That's because they answer to the gun lobbyists who sign their checks -- no matter how many innocent people die each and every day at the hands of their inaction. Enough is enough. We must call out what happened in El Paso and Dayton for what they are: domestic terrorism. It's a threat to the United States, and we've seen its devastating toll this weekend. We need to call out the president himself for advancing racism and white supremacy. And then we need to take action. It's powerfully important that we support Democrats across the country and take back the Senate in 2020 -- so we can take power back from Republicans, fight back against the gun lobby, and address the gun violence epidemic head on. "" Thanks for being a part of this fight, Elizabeth Warren "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2019 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02116

name: Ed Markey
subject: We cannot continue to live with the bloodshed and death of mass shootings
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
| |
---
| Hello,

In the face of two mass shootings in 24 hours, _**[I am calling on Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to stop blocking gun safety legislation.](http://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1dc56/693b8b0a/46be8d5b/72de8670/881287624/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-action-to-end-gun-violence?source=EM-080519_1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**_

We cannot continue to live with the bloodshed and death of mass shootings.

Mitch McConnell has blocked every gun reform legislation while taking millions of dollars in campaign donations from the NRA.

The majority of Americans support commonsense solutions to end gun violence. The only thing standing in our way are leaders who are in the pockets of gun manufacturers.

_**[Sign my petition and call on Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to bring the Senate back in session to vote on commonsense gun safety legislation.](http://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1dc56/693b8b0a/46be8d5b/72de8670/881287624/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-action-to-end-gun-violence?source=EM-080519_1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**_

In Massachusetts, we took matters into our own hands.

We identified the problems of gun violence, put our communities and public safety officers first, and passed gun reform laws.

We now have the lowest level of gun deaths in the country. This is not a coincidence.

We need reforms at the national level, and Mitch McConnell must stop blocking commonsense solutions on behalf of the NRA.

_**[If you agree, sign my petition and call on Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to bring the Senate back in session to vote on commonsense gun safety legislation now.](http://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1dc56/693b8b0a/46be8d5b/72de8670/881287624/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-action-to-end-gun-violence?source=EM-080519_1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**_

Thanks for standing with me,

Ed

name: =?utf-8?Q?Ben_Ray_Luj=C3=A1n?=
subject: domestic terrorism
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by People for Ben
body:
---
| | The horrific mass shooting the El Paso community suffered this weekend was an act of domestic terrorism and white supremacy. My heart breaks for the victims in El Paso, in Dayton, and other communities across the country who lost loved ones due to gun violence in the past week. We cannot sit by while our communities are ripped apart by gun violence. I'm asking you to call these vile acts by their names. I'm asking you to confront the hatred that took the lives of dozens of Americans. **And then, I 'm asking you to join me in doing something about it.** Whether it's giving blood, or giving to an organization dedicated to ending gun violence, please consider taking action at any of the links below:
* **[American Red Cross](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde963/1334397430/VEsH/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.redcrossblood.org/")**
* **[Giffords - Courage to Fight Gun Violence](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde960/1334397430/VEsE/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://giffords.org/")**
* **[Moms Demand Action for Gun Sense in America](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde961/1334397430/VEsF/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://momsdemandaction.org/")**
* **[Brady Campaign to Prevent Gun Violence](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde966/1334397430/VEsC/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.bradyunited.org/")**

House Democrats and I sent Mitch McConnell legislation to prevent gun violence more than 3 months ago. I've watched him do everything in his power to keep that legislation from getting a vote. All the while, the carnage of innocent life is in danger of becoming an everyday-norm. **I will fight with every breath I have to ensure that does not become the case.** I'm asking you to fight with me. It has become abundantly clear that Republicans have nothing but thoughts and prayers to offer in response to these horrors. So please join me in supporting organizations that are actually doing something to end this hatred and horrific loss of life. Thank you, Ben Ray
---
|
---
[](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde967/1334397430/VEsD/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.facebook.com/BenRayLujan") [](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde964/1334397430/VEsA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://twitter.com/benraylujan") [](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde965/1334397430/VEsB/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.instagram.com/benraylujan/?hl=en")
Ben Ray Lujan is running to be the first Hispanic Senator from New Mexico in more than 40 years. No one like Ben Ray has ever run for Senate before. He led the charge to take back the House for the American people and Democrats (and succeeded by a historic margin) and now he's running to take back the Senate too.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because Ben Ray isn't taking a cent of corporate PAC money. His campaign is 100% people-powered. And the polls show we have a once-in-a-lifetime shot to finally defeat Mitch McConnell and take back the Senate. But we can't do this without winning New Mexico. Will you be a part of this historic movement?
**[DONATE](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde91a/1334397430/VEsO/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/brl_email?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**
We're counting on you to stick with us for the long-haul. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, [_**please let us know.**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde91b/1334397430/VEsP/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/fewer-emails") If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe [_here_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f9/18e32a7f/54822f43/7bcde919/1334397430/VEsHBQ/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/unsubscribe"), and we'll make sure to process your request.

name: Dan Baer
subject: RE: gun violence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
I'm furious that Donald Trump has failed to address the nation and condemn domestic terrorism and white supremacists.
|
---

| | I want to be perfectly clear: This is on them.

We've witnessed two mass shootings within less than 24 hours.

And a common sense gun reform bill -- with bipartisan support for background checks -- is being blocked from the Senate floor by Mitch McConnell.

**We can 't continue to live like this.**

[**I 'm demanding that Mitch McConnell call the Senate back into session to pass this vital background checks bill. I need 5,000 signatures on my petition before midnight -- and I'll personally deliver it to Mitch McConnell and demand he take action. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12198/542606179/VEsH/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-gun-reform-bill-petition-2?source=20190805_em_pet_b1_baer_all")

I grew up about a mile from Columbine.

I'm devastated that mass shootings like this can still happen in our country.

I'm furious that Donald Trump has failed to address the nation and condemn domestic terrorism and white supremacists.

**And I refuse to let Mitch McConnell stall instead of taking action to prevent needless tragedies like this happening again and again.**

I won't take this sitting down -- and you can't either.

Change will only happen if we all rise up in unprecedented numbers and demand Mitch McConnell pass this bill.

[**I 'm personally asking you to sign my petition demanding Mitch McConnell bring the Senate back to DC and pass Democrats' common sense gun reform bill. If we hit 5,000 signatures before midnight I will personally deliver this petition to McConnell. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12198/542606179/VEsE/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-gun-reform-bill-petition-2?source=20190805_em_pet_b1_baer_all")

I have one more favor to ask.

Please consider making a donation to the victims of the El Paso and Dayton shootings.

Together we can build a future without gun violence.

**El Paso Victims ' Fund: [https://payments.epcf.org/victims](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12199/542606179/VEsF/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://payments.epcf.org/victims")
Dayton Foundation: [https://www.daytonfoundation.org/](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a1219e/542606179/VEsC/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.daytonfoundation.org/")**

Thanks for taking a stand,

Dan
| [**SIGN YOUR NAME >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12198/542606179/VEsD/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-gun-reform-bill-petition-2?source=20190805_em_pet_b1_baer_all")
---

I remember the panic I felt when the Columbine school shooting happened in my hometown of Littleton, Colorado.

I was terrified that something happened to my siblings.

We were lucky that our family wasn't broken that day.

But it breaks my heart knowing how many parents, siblings, and families have suffered since then because we've failed to take action to end the gun violence epidemic.

This is personal to me -- and I promise I won't stop fighting until no American ever endures a needless tragedy like this again.

[**SIGN YOUR NAME >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12198/542606179/VEsA/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-gun-reform-bill-petition-2?source=20190805_em_pet_b1_baer_all")
---

---
[](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a1219f/542606179/VEsB/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/") [](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a1219c/542606179/VEsO/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://twitter.com/danbbaer")

Colorado is the most important Senate race in the entire country according to Nate Silver and CNN. It's our only chance of flipping the Senate. And to win the most important Senate race, we need the most qualified candidate.

Dan served as an Ambassador under President Obama and as the head of the Colorado Department of Higher Education in Governor Hickenlooper's cabinet. If elected, he would be the first openly gay man to serve in Senate history.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but it's because we're running a grassroots campaign and rejecting corporate PAC money. We need your help to win -- and grassroots donations like yours truly make the difference. | | [**DONATE**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a1219d/542606179/VEsP/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=footer&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")
---
If you'd like to receive fewer emails, please let us know [here](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12182/542606179/VEsHBQ/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/fewer-emails"). We hope you'll consider sticking with us long-term. But if you'd like to unsubscribe, please [click here](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827bd/232dfd1/9df432f/62a12183/542606179/VEsHBA/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/unsubscribe").

| | Paid for by Dan for Colorado

name: Ryan HQ
subject: how you can help the victims in Dayton
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
Last night, Tim attended a vigil in Dayton for the victims of this weekend's tragic mass shooting in Ohio. There, he shared words of support and condolences and called on Congress to reconvene to take immediate and overdue action to prevent this kind of gun violence from happening ever again.

**That 's why we're asking all of our supporters to call their Senators now and demand they reconvene to find solutions to the gun violence epidemic. Call the Capitol switchboard at (202) 224-3121 to be connected to your Senator's office immediately.**

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: Bloody August weekend.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
Friend,
I went to bed on Saturday night with the community of El Paso on my mind. When I awoke to the news of the second shooting in Dayton, I was shaken. Until we put action behind our thoughts and prayers these won’t be the last mass killings at the hands of a hate-filled gunman who should never have had access to assault weapons.
Friend, our government has failed us. Even after Sandy Hook, even after Parkland, even after Las Vegas, our leaders refuse to lead.
We deserve a government that responds when these shootings occur — and I don’t mean on Twitter. We deserve a Senate that isn’t controlled by Mitch McConnell and the NRA.
That is what is on the line in this election.
Last week it was Gilroy, this week it was El Paso and Dayton. When white supremacists vandalized the Temple Israel synagogue in Columbus, Georgia last week with anti-Semitic fliers, they could just as easily have come with assault weapons. And through all of this turmoil, our senator remains silent about this threat to Temple Israel — when synagogues in Pittsburgh and Poway have been targeted for mass shootings in just the last year — and offers only thoughts and prayers to the families of the slain in Texas and Ohio.
Friend, in spite of mind-boggling Senate inaction by Mitch McConnell and David Perdue, you can help do something right now to counteract the scourge of gun violence.
I am asking you to split a $5 contribution between Moms Demand Action and Giffords PAC so that we can begin to take steps right now to counteract gun violence and win this race to enact real gun safety legislation that will save lives. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gun_violence?refcode=em080519]
Thank you for joining me and taking this important stand.
—Teresa
P.S.: You can read the Daily Beast op-ed I wrote last year about gun violence and Georgia here. [https://www.thedailybeast.com/in-georgia-its-the-nra-and-the-legislators-vs-the-police]

subject: Needed: Serious action to prevent gun violence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
While families across our nation are in mourning after the horrific, devastating mass shootings over the weekend, one fact remains: thoughts and prayers are simply not enough. We need Mitch McConnell to act.

[**If you agree, then stand with me to pass legislation to prevent what happened over the weekend from happening again.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/MVrPiSVh26b4phtcj0bVW_LlMYnrX3j4Q9XN5t2ZJbw=/rHB9S_BqncAUWz5khkvdPLobXbPqU4-vlmkO33uMhus=)

**The rate of gun homicide in America is six times higher than in Canada and almost 20 times the rate in Australia. **

The time has come for Washington to enact sensible gun safety laws and regulations: universal background checks, bans on high capacity semi-automatic weapons, and the restriction of straw purchases to end the epidemic of violence in our nation.

Children shouldn't be scared to go to school. Parents should never be fearful when they drop their child off at school that may be the last time they see them alive. Families shouldn't have to worry for their lives when they go back to school shopping. Friends shouldn't have to be fearful to relax and enjoy spending time together in public gathering places.

These things are NOT too much to ask. Thoughts and prayers are not enough. It's time to act. Stand with me in telling Congress and the President to pass real reforms now.

Thank you,

Sri Preston Kulkarni

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump: ‘Hate has no place in America’
body:
Earlier today, President Donald J. Trump addressed the nation from the White House. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=889a22d432&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • August 5, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump: ‘Hate has no place in America’**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=675d5638a4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **On Saturday morning** , a wicked person opened fire on innocent families shopping at a Walmart store in El Paso, Texas. Precious children were among the victims. The following day, another act of evil took place on a crowded street in Dayton, Ohio. These two horrible incidents left more than 80 people killed or wounded.

**Earlier today,** President Donald J. Trump addressed the nation from the White House:
| | _The First Lady and I join all Americans in praying and grieving for the victims, their families, and the survivors. We will stand by their side forever. We will never forget. These barbaric slaughters are an assault upon our communities, an attack upon our nation, and a crime against all of humanity. We are outraged and sickened by this monstrous evil, the cruelty, the hatred, the malice, the bloodshed, and the terror. Our hearts are shattered for every family whose parents, children, husbands, and wives were ripped from their arms and their lives. America weeps for the fallen. We are a loving nation, and our children are entitled to grow up in a just, peaceful and loving society. Together, we lock arms to shoulder the grief. We ask God in Heaven to ease the anguish of those who suffer. And we vow to act with urgent resolve._ |
---|---|---

**Hate,** in any of its forms, has no place in the United States of America. In the two decades since Columbine, our nation has witnessed one mass shooting after another. It’s easy to feel powerless in the face of such violence. America can and will rise to the challenge.

**By taking action** , “we will ensure that those who were attacked will not have died in vain,” President Trump said. He laid out those steps today:
* First, Americans must come together in condemning racism, bigotry, and white supremacy. “These sinister ideologies must be defeated,” the President said.

* Second, law enforcement must have all the tools it needs to investigate and disrupt any hate crimes or acts of domestic terrorism. President Trump has asked the FBI to identify any additional resources needed to confront these threats. Part of that effort includes fighting radicalization online.

* Third, America must do a better job of identifying—and acting upon—early warning signs of violence. Today, President Trump directed the Department of Justice to partner with government agencies and the private sector, including social media companies, to develop tools to detect mass shooters before they act.

* Fourth, we must stop the glorification of violence across society. It’s far too easy for troubled individuals to surround themselves with gruesome, grisly images on a daily basis. “Cultural change is hard, but each of us can choose to build a culture that celebrates the inherent worth and dignity of every human life,” the President said.

* Fifth, our country must reform its mental health laws to better identify, treat, and—if necessary—confine individuals who may commit acts of violence.

* Last but not least, we must ensure that those posing a great risk to public safety do not have access to firearms—and that, if they do, those firearms can be taken away through rapid due process. The President has called for “red flag laws,” also known as extreme risk protection orders, to keep weapons away from dangerous people.

**These steps** build upon important work that President Trump has already done to address the scourge of mass violence in America. Last year, Republicans and Democrats joined together to pass the STOP School Violence and Fix NICS Acts, providing grants to improve school safety and strengthening critical background checks for firearms. On the President’s orders, the Department of Justice also banned the sale of bump stocks.

**Today** , President Trump also called upon DOJ to propose legislation that ensures anyone who commits hate crimes and mass murder will face the death penalty.

**“Now is the time** to set destructive partisanship aside . . . and find the courage to answer hatred with unity, devotion, and love,” the President said. “Our future is in our control.”

[President Trump’s Proclamation honoring the victims in El Paso and Dayton. ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f71fa34173&e=3ee4985eea)

clean_body: Earlier today, President Donald J. Trump addressed the nation from the White House. | | | | | The White House • August 5, 2019 | | | President Trump: ‘Hate has no place in America’ | | On Saturday morning , a wicked person opened fire on innocent families shopping at a Walmart store in El Paso, Texas. Precious children were among the victims. The following day, another act of evil took place on a crowded street in Dayton, Ohio. These two horrible incidents left more than 80 people killed or wounded. Earlier today, President Donald J. Trump addressed the nation from the White House: | | The First Lady and I join all Americans in praying and grieving for the victims, their families, and the survivors. We will stand by their side forever. We will never forget. These barbaric slaughters are an assault upon our communities, an attack upon our nation, and a crime against all of humanity. We are outraged and sickened by this monstrous evil, the cruelty, the hatred, the malice, the bloodshed, and the terror. Our hearts are shattered for every family whose parents, children, husbands, and wives were ripped from their arms and their lives. America weeps for the fallen. We are a loving nation, and our children are entitled to grow up in a just, peaceful and loving society. Together, we lock arms to shoulder the grief. We ask God in Heaven to ease the anguish of those who suffer. And we vow to act with urgent resolve. | ---|---|--- Hate, in any of its forms, has no place in the United States of America. In the two decades since Columbine, our nation has witnessed one mass shooting after another. It’s easy to feel powerless in the face of such violence. America can and will rise to the challenge. By taking action , “we will ensure that those who were attacked will not have died in vain,” President Trump said. He laid out those steps today: First, Americans must come together in condemning racism, bigotry, and white supremacy. “These sinister ideologies must be defeated,” the President said. Second, law enforcement must have all the tools it needs to investigate and disrupt any hate crimes or acts of domestic terrorism. President Trump has asked the FBI to identify any additional resources needed to confront these threats. Part of that effort includes fighting radicalization online. Third, America must do a better job of identifying—and acting upon—early warning signs of violence. Today, President Trump directed the Department of Justice to partner with government agencies and the private sector, including social media companies, to develop tools to detect mass shooters before they act. Fourth, we must stop the glorification of violence across society. It’s far too easy for troubled individuals to surround themselves with gruesome, grisly images on a daily basis. “Cultural change is hard, but each of us can choose to build a culture that celebrates the inherent worth and dignity of every human life,” the President said. Fifth, our country must reform its mental health laws to better identify, treat, and—if necessary—confine individuals who may commit acts of violence. Last but not least, we must ensure that those posing a great risk to public safety do not have access to firearms—and that, if they do, those firearms can be taken away through rapid due process. The President has called for “red flag laws,” also known as extreme risk protection orders, to keep weapons away from dangerous people. These steps build upon important work that President Trump has already done to address the scourge of mass violence in America. Last year, Republicans and Democrats joined together to pass the STOP School Violence and Fix NICS Acts, providing grants to improve school safety and strengthening critical background checks for firearms. On the President’s orders, the Department of Justice also banned the sale of bump stocks. Today , President Trump also called upon DOJ to propose legislation that ensures anyone who commits hate crimes and mass murder will face the death penalty. “Now is the time to set destructive partisanship aside . . . and find the courage to answer hatred with unity, devotion, and love,” the President said. “Our future is in our control.” Watch: | | Photo of the Day | Official White House Photo by Tia Dufour | The United States flag flies at half-staff atop the White House in solemn respect for the victims of the mass shootings in El Paso, Texas, and Dayton, Ohio | August 4, 2019 | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
